input.background: A study of environmental air quality measured suspended particulate matter in air samples at two sites.
input.data:
Table 12.9 "Air samples data" for Exercises 12.5 and 12.7. Data are suspended particulate matter in air samples ( $\mu \mathrm{gms} / \mathrm{m}^3$ ).

\begin{tabular}{lllllllll}
\hline Site 1: & 68 & 22 & 36 & 32 & 42 & 24 & 28 & 38 \\
Site 2: & 36 & 38 & 39 & 40 & 36 & 34 & 33 & 32 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Data are fictitious and are stored online in the file AIRSAMPLES.SAV.
input.question: Create back-to-back stemplots of data from these sites. Discuss the results.

output.answer:
Stemplots
```
Site 1| |\mathrm{ Site 2}
842 |2 |
862 |3 |2346689
2|4|0
    |5|
    8|6|
    \times10
```

Discussion:
- Site 1 has a high outlier.
- The distributions have similar central locations.
- Site 1 has greater variability.

site_1_particulate_matter.description: Suspended particulate matter measurements (in μg/m³) from air samples at Site 1.

site_2_particulate_matter.description: Suspended particulate matter measurements (in μg/m³) from air samples at Site 2.
